_comment: Cleanup target catalog for the storage module. Each source: name, description, path (env vars expanded at runtime), filter (glob), minAgeDays (skip files newer than N days so active work isn't nuked), riskLevel (auto = delete without asking, ask = confirm because it's big or holds recoverable data, skip_if_unused_apps_ran = another module already used this), reclaimEstimate (rough MB, 0 = unknown until diagnose scans it).

sources.name: user_temp
sources.description: Per-user %TEMP% — installer leftovers, half-decompressed archives, IDE caches.

sources.name: local_appdata_temp
sources.description: AppData\Local\Temp — same purpose as %TEMP%, often a duplicate location.

sources.name: windows_temp
sources.description: System-wide C:\Windows\Temp — service and installer scratch.

sources.name: windows_update_cache
sources.description: Windows Update download cache (SoftwareDistribution\Download).
sources.notes: Stop wuauserv before purging, restart after.

sources.name: delivery_optimization_cache
sources.description: Delivery Optimization P2P update chunks.
sources.notes: Prefer 'Delete-DeliveryOptimizationCache' cmdlet on Win11 if available.

sources.name: recycle_bin
sources.description: System-wide recycle bin (all drives).
sources.notes: Use Clear-RecycleBin -Force cmdlet, not direct delete. Ask user — may hold recoverable files.

sources.name: windows_old
sources.description: C:\Windows.old — previous OS install after a feature update / upgrade.
sources.notes: Can be 15-40 GB. Deletion is one-way — after this the 10-day rollback is gone.

sources.name: thumbnail_cache
sources.description: Explorer thumbnail cache DBs — regenerated on demand.
sources.notes: Stop Explorer first, or the DBs are locked.

sources.name: iconcache
sources.description: Explorer icon cache DBs — regenerated on demand.

sources.name: crash_dumps_user
sources.description: Per-user CrashDumps folder.
sources.notes: Ask if crashdumps module hasn't run yet — the dumps may be useful for diagnosis.

sources.name: minidumps
sources.description: Kernel minidumps.
sources.notes: Same as crash_dumps_user — coordinate with crashdumps module.

sources.name: memory_dump
sources.description: Full/kernel memory dump (MEMORY.DMP) from a BSOD.
sources.notes: Can be several GB. Ask before deleting.

sources.name: prefetch
sources.description: Windows Prefetch — used by SysMain to speed app launches.
sources.notes: The unused-apps module reads Prefetch to detect unlaunched apps. If that module ran this session, leave Prefetch alone.

sources.name: font_cache
sources.description: Windows Font cache — regenerated by FontCache service.

sources.name: logs_wer
sources.description: Windows Error Reporting queued/archived reports.

sources.name: logs_cbs
sources.description: Component-Based Servicing logs (grow after every DISM/SFC run).

sources.name: logs_dism
sources.description: DISM logs.

sources.name: edge_cache
sources.description: Microsoft Edge web cache — regenerated on use.
sources.notes: Ask — user may have offline cached content.

sources.name: chrome_cache
sources.description: Google Chrome web cache — regenerated on use.

sources.name: dism_component_cleanup
sources.description: Not a directory — invoke DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /StartComponentCleanup /ResetBase to shrink WinSxS.
sources.notes: ResetBase is one-way — cannot uninstall installed superseded Windows Updates afterward. Ask user.
